PH*WY*'I PI*JMH : t , *rci ^jn fnef^-f %^fN froER ^t <ra& faPi:^ % %* [ T O R 1-1/2003 'af^wfto-1 ] NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 22nd September, 2'.)()3 S.O. 1092(E).—In exercise of Ihepowers conferred by sub-section (2) and (3) of section 5A read with section 63 of the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 (53 of 1972), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules. namely :— 1 Short title and commencement— (1) These rules may be called the National Board for Wild Life Rules, 2003. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette t. D efi nit ions. - In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires,— (a) ""Act" means the Wild Life (Protection) Act. 1972 (53 of 1972); (b) "National Board" means the National Board for Wild Life constituted under section 5 A of the Act; 4. 5. (c) "Chairperson"' means the Chairperson of the National Board; (d) all other words and expressions used in these rules shall have the meanings respectively assigned to them in the Act. National Board for Wild Life.— (1) The National Board shall be constituted in accordance with the provisions contained in sub-section (1) of section 5 A of the Act (2) The term of office of die members other than those who are members ex-officio shall be for a period of three years. (3) The Member of Parliament nominated to the National Board shall cease to be the Member of the National Board on his having ceased to the Member of Parliament. (4) The vacancies referred to in clauses (e), (f) and (v) of sub-section (1) of section SAofthe Act shall be filled by nomination by the Central Government with die approval of Chairperson Meetings.-— (1) The National Board shall ordinarily meet once in a year at a place to be decided with the approval of the Chairperson. (2) The Member-Secretary shall prepare agenda items for the meetings, obtain approval of the Chairperson and circulate it to all members at least fifteen days prior to me date of such meeting. (3) The Member-Secretary shall prepare the minutes of the meeting and circulate it to all members within thirty days of the meeting after obtaining approval of the Chairperson. (4) The Member-Secretary shall also initiate follow op action on the decisions of the National Board. Removal from the National Board.— The Central Government may, with the prior approval of the Chairperson, remove any member of the National Board appointed under clause (e), (f) and -- 3 of 7 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PARTlI-isrr?(ii)) 8. (v) of sub-section (1) of section 5 A of the Act, if— (a) he is of unsound mind and stands so declared by a competent court; or be Is an undischarged insolvent; or <b) (c) (d) he is convicted of any offence including moral turpitude, or his conduct is found to be unbecoming of a member Disclosure of interest by m e m b e r s — If jjfmg Member of the National Board or his family member has any interest a»B#roposal submii (ed for ^ J ^ j ^ S P h e n of the N>#anal Board, the member shall dj*close the aQ&cf^tyvpjn the proposal l>l^ittii>Vtr<hein<>etiiiyrf the National Board.— (1) The Chairperson or in his absence the Vice- Griai^iiwlJiftlHl^rtsidc at every meeting of theNaJknalBoard (2) In the absence of both the Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson, such other member present as is decided by the National Board shall preside over the meeting Quorum.— (1) The quorum necessary for the transaction of business at a meeting of the National Board shall be one-half of the total number of members (2) If at any time there is no quorum, the Chairperson or the person presiding over a meeting shall adjourn the meeting until there i s a quorum. 9. Invitees to the National Board meetings.— The Chairperson of the National Board may invite any person or persons having experience in th e wild life conservation to attend any meeting of the National Board. 10. Record of business.— (1) The record of business transaction al every meeting of the National Board shall be prepared by the Member-Secretary for approval by the Chairperson or t h e Member presiding at such meeting (2) When any business is transacted by circulation of paper*. the M«mbcr-Sccm*n shall prepare a record of the business so transacted for anproval by the Clumpersou of the National Board permitting the circulatiori (3) The record of business transacted at every meeting of the National B o a r d shall be approved and signed by the ChaiqMqsejj or the Vice^Chairperson or the member r e s i d i n g at such meeting, and the a m o v e d record of b u s i a e i s i l ^ b e c i r c a h y e d t o aU themcanbers (4) All records shall be m a i n t a i n e d by the Member-Secretary of items o f business transacted by the National B o a r d or the committees, sub-committees or sub-groups thereof 11. Appointment of Committees.— (1) The National Board m a y , c o n s t i t u t e a Standinig C o m m i t t e e a n d s u c h other committees ©r study groups, as may be necessary, in accordance with t h e provisions contained in section 5B of the A c t (2) The National Board shall determine the terms of reference, tenure, membership and other related aspects as may be necessary in respect of the committees constituted u n d e r sub-rule (I) of this rule. 12. Travellhig and other allowances to m e m b e r s of the National Board and its committees.— • • ' " • • ' . . . • (1) A member of the National B o a r d or any aDrnrnittee other than members ex-offiao, shall be entitled to receive, in respect of any journey performed by him for the purpose of attending a meeting of uie National Board or of a duly constituted cofljinittee thereof or for the purpose of discharging any duty assigned to him by the National Board or t h e committee concerned, travelling allowances and daily allowances at the rates as are admissible to the Group ' A ' officers of t h e Central Government for the time being in force -- 4 of 7 -- •«*inll—mo? 3(a) "ira sri J 3. No (ravelling allowance or daily allowance shall be allowed to a member of the National Board or of any conunittce unless he certifies that he has noi drawn any travelling or daily allowance from any other source in respect of the journey and halt for which the claim is (3) The travelling allowance shall be payable from the usual place of residence of a member of die National Board or any of (he committee to the place of the meeting or the place where he has gone to attend to any business of the National Board and back to his place ol residence (4) In special circumstances, (he Chairperson may grant travelling allowance from die places other than usual place of residence of a Interpretation of rules.— • • iiCrc any QOUnt arssco as to tiic iiiicrprctaiion Oi diese rules, interpretation shall be referred to the Central Governrnesitfor its IF No 1-1/2003' VTNOD RISHI. O. 1093 (E).—In exercise of thepowers conferral by Sub-section (1) of Section 5 A of the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 (53 of 1972), the Central Government hereby constitutes the National Board for Wild Life consisting of the following members, namely:— (a) Prime Minister Chairperson (b) Minister in-charge of Forests Vice-Cliairperson and Wild Life (c) The following Members of Pari lament Members (i) Shri Rajen Gohain, Lok Sabha Cii) Shri Khelsai Singh, Lok Sabha (iii) Smt. Maya Singh, Rajva Sabha (d) Member, Planning Commission in- Member charge of Forests and Wild Life (e) The heads of the following non- Members governmental organisations: (i) Bandhavgarh Foundation Trust, Rewa, Madhya Pradesh (ii) Prakrity Samsad, Kolkata, West Bengal (hi) Foundation for Revitalisation of Local Health Traditions, Bangalore (iv) CPRamaswamy Environmental Education Centre, Chennai, T.N (v) Gujarat Ecological Educationand Research Foundation, Gujarat (f) The following eminent conservationists, Members ecologies and environmentalists — (i) Shri Mir inayatullah (ii) Shri Saketanand Singh (iii) Shri Samar Singh (iv) Shri Raj Chengappa (v) Shri Ravi Singh (vi) Shri Mithilesh K Sharma (vii) Shri R.L.
